name: makerchip
description: VS Code extension for Makerchip IDE integration

makerchip.autoUpdateResources.description: Automatically update Makerchip reference data on activation

enumDescriptions: Ask whether to add reference data folder to workspace
Always add reference data folder to workspace
Never add reference data folder to workspace
makerchip.addResourcesFolder.description: Add Makerchip reference data folder to workspace (for LLMs)

makerchip.serverUrl.description: Base URL of the Makerchip server (for testing with local or staging servers)

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_compile
languageModelTools.userDescription: Compile and open TL-Verilog in Makerchip IDE.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Opens IDE and compiles TL-Verilog code. **Returns immediately** - compilation is async. Returns compile ID and cache paths. Poll `~/.vscode-makerchip/compile-cache/<ID>/metadata.json` for status (check 'complete', 'passed', 'error' fields). Use this when user wants to VISUALIZE TL-Verilog, not just see code.

filePath.description: Optional path to a TL-Verilog file to open and compile. If not provided, uses the currently active editor.

code.description: Optional TL-Verilog code to compile. When provided, creates a new unsaved file with this code and opens it in Makerchip. Use this to show examples or demonstrate TL-Verilog features.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. Default: `default`. Use this to open multiple Makerchip panels for different designs or comparisons.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_ide_call
languageModelTools.userDescription: Call any Makerchip IDE method directly.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generic tool for calling any IDE Plugin API method directly. Many of these methods are explicitly exposed as tools. For full IDE Plugin details, see `.vscode-makerchip/resources/Makerchip-public/docs/plugin_api/index.html`. Note that methods like onReady, onCodeChange, onCompilationLog are callbacks and should not be called directly.

args.description: Arguments to pass to the method. Order matters.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. Default: `default`.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_get_viz_image
languageModelTools.userDescription: Capture the current VIZ visualization as an image.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the current Makerchip VIZ (Visual Debug) canvas visualization (from an open IDE panel with compiled/simulated code). Returns a base64-encoded data URL. Use this to iterate on VIZ development.

quality.description: Image quality for jpeg/webp formats (0.0-1.0). Default is 1.0.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. Default: `default`.

saveToFile.description: Optional: save image to file for user visibility. If true, saves to /tmp with auto-generated name. If string, uses as file path. Image data is always returned to the model.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_get_layout_state
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get the current IDE pane layout configuration.
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Retrieves the current Makerchip IDE layout state as a serializable object from an open IDE panel. The returned data structure refects the recursive binary tree of splits and the tabbed panes at leaf splits, identified by their mnemonics.

Use this to: capture current layout for later restoration, understand current IDE configuration, or create modified layouts based on the current state. The returned object can be passed directly to makerchip_set_layout_state or modified before restoration.

SEE: `.vscode-makerchip/.vscode/skills/makerchip-api-features.md` for details about working with layouts and panes

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. Default: `default`.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_set_layout_state
languageModelTools.userDescription: Configure the IDE pane layout (create splits, arrange panes in tabs, set active pane).

state.description: Layout state defining pane arrangement (TabbedView or FlexSplit)

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. Default: `default`.

panes.description: Array of pane mnemonics in tab order

oneOf.description: Horizontal split with left and right sides
Vertical split with top and bottom sides

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_get_available_panes
languageModelTools.userDescription: List all available IDE panes with descriptions.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns all panes with mnemonics (name[+uniquifier]), display names, descriptions, and availability. Main panes: Log, Nav-TLV, Diagram, Viz, Waveform. Static panes: Tutorials, Examples, Courses. Third-party panes if any. Only use panes with available=true in layout operations.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. Default: `default`.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_open_pane
languageModelTools.userDescription: Open or activate a specific IDE pane by mnemonic.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Opens and/or activates a pane by mnemonic in an open IDE panel. Works with static panes (Tutorials, Examples, opened on demand), main panes (Log, Diagram, etc.), and third-party panes. Use makerchip_get_available_panes for valid mnemonics. For complex layouts use makerchip_set_layout_state.

mnemonic.description: The mnemonic (unique identifier) of the pane to open or activate. Use makerchip_get_available_panes to discover valid mnemonics.

background.description: If true, opens the pane in the background without activating it. Default is false (activate the pane).

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. Default: `default`.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_open_third_party_pane
languageModelTools.userDescription: Open custom content (PDFs, web pages) in IDE panes.

mnemonic.description: Display name for the pane (tab label). Will be uniquified automatically if a pane with this name already exists.

contentType.description: Type of content to display: 'pdf' for PDF viewer or 'iframe' for embedded web content.

contentUrl.description: URL to the content. For PDFs: supports https://, http://, file://, vscode-webview://, data URIs. For iframes: must support iframe embedding. Provide this OR filePath.

filePath.description: Absolute path to a local file to load. The extension reads the file and builds a data: URI internally, so large file contents never pass through the model context. Preferred over contentUrl for local files. Takes precedence when both are provided.

contentParams.description: Content parameters. Provide either contentUrl or filePath.

background.description: If true, opens the pane without activating it (leaves current pane active). Default is false.

nonTransferrable.description: If true, pane construction data won't be stored for layout recovery. Use for temporary panes. Default is false.

panelName.description: Optional VS Code panel name to target. Default: `default`.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_get_cycle
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns active cycle value. All "live" panes (Nav-TLV, Diagram, VIZ, Waveform) display data for this cycle. Cycle 0 is first cycle after reset.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. Default: `default`.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_set_cycle

cycle.description: Absolute cycle to jump to (0-based). Mutually exclusive with offset. Final value clamped to [0, endCycle].

offset.description: Relative offset from current cycle (can be negative). Mutually exclusive with cycle.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. Default: `default`.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_update_play_state
languageModelTools.userDescription: Start or stop waveform playback animation.
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Controls waveform playback animation (cycle advancement). Starts or stops playback, optionally setting cycle range and playback speed.

When starting (isPlaying=true):
- Advances active cycle automatically at specified interval (cycleTimeout, default 1000ms)
- Optionally jumps to startCyc before starting
- Stops at endCyc if provided, otherwise plays to end of waveform
- Updates all live panes (Nav-TLV, Diagram, VIZ, Waveform) each cycle

When stopping (isPlaying=false):
- Stops cycle advancement at current position
- Other parameters ignored

EXAMPLES:
- Start playback from beginning at 500ms/cycle: {"isPlaying": true, "cycleTimeout": 500, "startCyc": 0}
- Play cycles 10-50 at default speed: {"isPlaying": true, "startCyc": 10, "endCyc": 50}
- Stop playback: {"isPlaying": false}

isPlaying.description: Whether to start (true) or stop (false) waveform playback.

cycleTimeout.description: Delay in milliseconds between cycles during playback. Default is 1000ms. Only used when isPlaying=true.

startCyc.description: Starting cycle for playback. If provided, jumps to this cycle before starting. Only used when isPlaying=true.

endCyc.description: Ending cycle for playback. If provided, playback stops at this cycle; otherwise plays to end of waveform. Only used when isPlaying=true.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. Default: `default`.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_highlight
languageModelTools.userDescription: Highlight a signal, scope, or pipeline stage across all IDE views.
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Highlights all replicated instances of an entity across the IDE (Nav-TLV, Diagram, Waveform). If accumulate=false (default), replaces highlights. If true, adds to existing.

PATH SYNTAX (varies by type):
- Pipesignal: '/cpu|pipe$data' (full path)
- Scope: '/cpu', '/core|in/flit', or '/unit|reg' (full path)
- Stage: '|pipe@2' (not a full path; just pipeline and pipestage)

Never include range/index expressions, like `[2]`.
NEVER include M5 macros or `\TLV` macro parameters, like `|inst\m5_cnt` or `|_pipe`.

EXAMPLES: {"id": "/cpu|decode$valid"}, {"id": "|fetch@1", "accumulate": true}

id.description: The TL-Verilog path identifier to highlight (e.g., '/cpu|my_pipe$data', '|my_pipe@2', '/cpu')

accumulate.description: If true, adds to existing highlights (Ctrl+click behavior). If false (default), replaces all highlights.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. Default: `default`.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_clear_highlights
languageModelTools.userDescription: Clear all highlighted entities from IDE views.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Removes all entity highlights from all views: Diagram, Waveform, Nav-TLV panes.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. Default: `default`.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_set_dark_mode
languageModelTools.userDescription: Enable or disable dark mode in the Makerchip IDE.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Controls dark/light theme for all IDE views (editor, diagram, waveform). Pass true for dark mode, false for light mode. Updates localStorage and all panes immediately.

enabled.description: Enable dark mode (true) or light mode (false).

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. Default: `default`.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_set_live_mode
languageModelTools.userDescription: Enable or disable live mode for a specific pane (Diagram, Nav-TLV).
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Controls live/dead mode for panes that support it. Live panes auto-update as cycle changes during playback/stepping. Dead panes freeze at current cycle. Supported: Diagram, Nav-TLV. VIZ is always live. Returns true if successful, false if pane not found or doesn't support live mode.

mnemonic.description: The mnemonic of the pane to control (e.g., 'Diagram', 'Nav-TLV').

enabled.description: Enable live mode (true) or dead mode/freeze (false).

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. Default: `default`.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_list_panels
languageModelTools.userDescription: List all currently open Makerchip IDE panels.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns open panel names. Use these in 'panelName' parameter to target specific IDE instances. New panels are created only via makerchip_compile with a code or filePath argument.

chatParticipants.name: makerchip
chatParticipants.description: Makerchip IDE integration for TL-Verilog development

scripts.compile: tsc -p ./ && tsc -p src/tsconfig.webview.json && npm run copy-webview
scripts.watch: npm run copy-webview && (npm run watch:main & npm run watch:webview & npm run watch:html & wait)
scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
